channel.title: Tommy's Daily Tech Briefing
channel.description: Deep-dive technology news briefing with industry analysis, executive insights, and strategic context.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Wednesday, May 06, 2026
item.description: The Pentagon dropped $500M on Scale AI, OpenAI's Brockman survived a jury reading his diary, and Microsoft's green pledges are quietly losing to its data center hunger.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Tuesday, May 05, 2026
item.description: Apple courts Intel and Samsung to diversify from TSMC, the Musk-Altman trial goes full courtroom drama, and a cPanel zero-day is already ransoming half a million servers.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Monday, May 04, 2026
item.description: AI outdiagnosed ER doctors at triage, state health sites silently fed your location and immigration status to TikTok and Meta, and Block's free open-source Goose agent just undercut Anthropic's $200/month Claude Code.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Sunday, May 03, 2026
item.description: A Python script that roots any Linux box, a no-code squirrel guy's AI camera app hitting App Store #1, and Reddit beating earnings by $63M dominated today's tech news.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Saturday, May 02, 2026
item.description: OpenAI's dark-money influence campaign targeting Chinese AI rivals, the S&P hitting record highs, and Apple quietly axing its $599 Mac Mini as AI hardware costs squeeze consumers upward.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Friday, May 01, 2026
item.description: Musk's OpenAI trial exposed covert intelligence ties while Alphabet hit record highs on AI bets, and legal AI startup Legora hit $5.6B valuation mid-war with rival Harvey.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Thursday, April 30, 2026
item.description: Iranian drone strikes knocked out AWS UAE data centers while a critical Linux privilege-escalation flaw hit eight years of distros, and open-source AI keeps eating Big Tech's lunch.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Wednesday, April 29, 2026
item.description: AI may catch pancreatic cancer years before symptoms, BMW bets $300M on agentic car design, and hackers are now targeting security vendors Checkmarx and Bitwarden.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Tuesday, April 28, 2026
item.description: OpenAI broke from Microsoft to go multi-cloud, the Musk-Altman trial opened with $134B at stake, and Europe is actively ripping out US cloud infrastructure.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Monday, April 27, 2026
item.description: Musk and Altman head to court over OpenAI's future, DeepSeek torches AI pricing with a new flagship, and private capital floods fusion energy at record levels.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Sunday, April 26, 2026
item.description: SpaceX's $60B Cursor bid signals Musk's AI coding ambitions, while Apple crowns a hardware-first CEO and robots gain cross-platform skill-sharing — a day of big bets on who owns the future of building things.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Saturday, April 25, 2026
item.description: Google bet $40B on Anthropic, Artemis II astronauts broke a 56-year distance record, and Railway proved you can out-compete AWS with 30 people and no marketing budget.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Friday, April 24, 2026
item.description: GPT-5.5 redefines agentic AI, Europe bets €600M on Cohere over American models, and Altman vs. Musk finally hits court Sunday.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Thursday, April 23, 2026
item.description: Tesla's Full Self-Driving promises unraveled, a prediction market manipulation scheme sparked a criminal probe, and fusion energy investment leapt to $15B as AI quietly reshaped everything in between.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Wednesday, April 22, 2026
item.description: SpaceX eyes a $60B Cursor buyout while Elon keeps consolidating, NASA proves cheap 4K lunar comms are real, and IBM's AI bet flatlines at exactly analyst expectations.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Tuesday, April 21, 2026
item.description: Tim Cook steps down from Apple, handing the reins to John Ternus after fifteen years — plus rapid-fire moves across tech and markets.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Monday, April 20, 2026
item.description: Google pushes custom AI silicon, a major platform breach surfaces, and a shoe company decides it's a data center now.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Sunday, April 19, 2026
item.description: Salesforce rebuilds Slackbot from scratch, IPO stories turn heads, and the AI infrastructure arms race shows no signs of slowing.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Saturday, April 18, 2026
item.description: AI policy drama heats up, a high-profile CEO exits, a quantum computing billionaire appears overnight, and Netflix stumbles hard.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Friday, April 17, 2026
item.description: Google and CoreWeave pour billions into AI infrastructure, major model updates drop, and Apple-Google tensions reach new heights.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Thursday, April 16, 2026
item.description: OpenAI launches a cybersecurity model, chipmakers print record cash, and one of the stranger corporate pivots in recent memory.

item.title: Tech Briefing - Wednesday, April 15, 2026
item.description: AI liability risks emerge as Moody's flags lender exposure, data center arms race intensifies, and the industry's pace defies expectations.
